text.title: Agency Decline Templates Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding the Agency Decline Templates methodology. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Pack covers 5 templates: bad-fit, capacity, price, scope, timeline. Each names the decline reason explicitly.
rule.rationale: Pack covers 5 templates: bad-fit, capacity, price, scope, timeline. Each names the decline reason explicitly.

rule.statement: Each template names positioning in a single sentence (what you DO do). Avoids 'just not the right fit' without context.
rule.rationale: Each template names positioning in a single sentence (what you DO do). Avoids 'just not the right fit' without context.

rule.statement: Each template offers ≥1 named referral OR a public resource link. Pure decline = wasted lead.
rule.rationale: Each template offers ≥1 named referral OR a public resource link. Pure decline = wasted lead.

rule.statement: Edits bump semver; older sent emails remain traceable.
rule.rationale: Edits bump semver; older sent emails remain traceable.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent's context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-agency-decline-templates.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
